_meta.game: Donkey Kong Country 3: Dixie Kong's Double Trouble!

sources.name: Wikipedia — Donkey Kong Country 3
sources.notes: Mechanics overview. Kiddy Kong physics, Dixie helicopter spin, animal buddies.

sources.name: DKC Atlas — Donkey Kong Country 3 Studies
sources.notes: DKC series physics reference. DKC1/2/3 use same 8.8 fixed-point engine.

notes: DKC3 is the third game in Rare's DKC trilogy for SNES. Released 1996, one of the last major SNES titles.
USES SAME ENGINE AS DKC1 AND DKC2. Core physics constants assumed identical.
Main character: Dixie Kong (same as DKC2 secondary). Secondary: Kiddy Kong (new — large, powerful).
KIDDY KONG ADDITION: Water skipping. Kiddy can bounce along water surfaces instead of falling in (in specific contexts).
KIDDY KONG ADDITION: Ground roll. Kiddy's roll is different from DKC1/2 — larger body means it hits more enemies.
The helicopter spin from DKC2 (Dixie) is retained — same mechanic.
Features a large overworld map (Northern Kremisphere) with boat and vehicle traversal.

base_physics._note: Assumed same as DKC1/DKC2 engine. All values from DKC1 documentation apply.

jump_velocity_initial.source: DKC1 ROM-confirmed values — assumed same engine in DKC3. VERIFY.

gravity_holding_jump.notes: Dual gravity system from DKC1 — holding jump button applies this lower gravity.

gravity_not_holding.notes: Released jump button applies this higher gravity — signature DKC 'tight' fall.

dixie_kong._note: Dixie Kong is the PRIMARY character in DKC3 (was secondary in DKC2).

helicopter_hair_spin.description: Press and hold jump while airborne — Dixie's ponytail spins like a helicopter rotor, slowing descent.
helicopter_hair_spin.effect: Significantly reduced fall speed. Limited horizontal movement control during spin.
notes: Same mechanic as DKC2 — carried forward unchanged.
Allows crossing wide gaps and reaching elevated platforms inaccessible by standard jump.
The helicopter spin is the primary aerial traversal tool for Dixie.

cartwheel_attack.description: Spinning attack similar to DKC1/2 roll. Hits enemies in front.
cartwheel_attack.notes: Lighter than Kiddy's roll — less inertia, easier to control.

throw_kiddy.description: When Dixie is leading, she can throw Kiddy forward or upward to reach far/high platforms.
throw_kiddy.notes: The throw mechanic from DKC1 (Kong throwing Kong) — same system. Kong toss = traversal tool.

kiddy_kong._note: New character in DKC3. Toddler Kong — large, powerful, distinct mechanics.

water_skip.description: Kiddy can skip along water surfaces if rolling onto water at speed. Bounces several times before sinking.
notes: Unique to Kiddy — Dixie sinks immediately.
Allows crossing water hazards that would otherwise require a different route.
Number of skips depends on approach speed and timing.

roll_attack.description: Standard DKC roll, but Kiddy's large body gives it a slightly different feel.
roll_attack.notes: Larger hitbox than Dixie's cartwheel — may hit more enemies per use.

throw_dixie.description: When Kiddy is leading, he can throw Dixie upward to reach elevated platforms or far distances.
throw_dixie.notes: Cooperative traversal — one Kong can reach places the other cannot.

animal_buddies._note: DKC3 has new animal buddies not in DKC1/2.

ellie.ability: Suck up water with trunk, spray forward at enemies. Can also carry barrels.
notes: Ellie is afraid of mice — certain enemies with mouse traits will scare her.
The water-spray mechanic is new to DKC3 — creates puzzle-solving opportunities.

squitter.ability: Shoot webs that become temporary platforms. Same as DKC2.

parry.ability: Deflects projectiles automatically. Follows the Kongs and blocks incoming shots.
parry.notes: Unique to DKC3. Passive defense rather than active attack — changes how projectile enemies are handled.

benny_and_bjorn.notes: Vehicle traversal on the overworld map — not level-internal mechanics.

funky.description: Funky Kong operates a boat and jetski on the overworld.
funky.notes: Vehicle traversal tool for the Northern Kremisphere map.

overworld.description: Large connected map (Northern Kremisphere) with lakes, forests, and mountains. Travel between areas requires Funky's boat or other vehicles.
notes: The overworld map is more complex than DKC1/2 — not just a world select screen.
Boat and vehicle sections add traversal variety on the world map itself.
Some areas are inaccessible without specific vehicles.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: DKC3's Kiddy Kong water-skipping mechanic is a perfect example of physics-based character differentiation: a single property (Kiddy bounces on water, Dixie doesn't) creates completely different level routing options. Same physics engine, different character hitbox/mass properties, wildly different traversal.

The helicopter spin carried forward from DKC2 to DKC3 demonstrates 'mechanic inheritance' — a beloved traversal tool becomes an expected part of the Dixie Kong identity. Players who learned it in DKC2 have an immediate fluency advantage in DKC3.

Ellie's fear of mice creates a vulnerability mechanic — normally indestructible animal buddies have a contextual weakness. This makes enemy placement design more interesting: a mouse near Ellie is a high-value threat, creating dynamic threat prioritization.

Parry the Parallel Bird (passive projectile deflection) shifts defensive strategy: instead of dodging projectiles, players let Parry handle them. This changes spatial attention — stop watching for incoming shots, start managing where Parry is relative to threats.

The Northern Kremisphere overworld map (with boat navigation) adds a layer of world exploration not present in DKC1/2. The world itself becomes a puzzle with gates and routes — the overworld map as its own game mode within the game.
